August 22,2000
Barbara Booth Grunwald
Deputy County Counsel
County of Fresno
2220TuIarc Street, Fifth Floor
Fresno, CA 93716
SUPERSEDED

Re
Your Request for Advice
Our Advice Letter No. A-00-183
Dear Ms. Grunwald:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the economic
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTION
May the Board of Supervisors issue a general letter to school districts and special
districts within Fresno County advising them that statements of economic interests for
"heads of agencies" for those districts may be retained by the district rather than being
submitted to the Board of Supervisors, as has been done in the past?
CONCLUSION
No. The statements of economic interests for county school and special districts
must be filed with the Fresno Count-v Board of Supervisors and made available to the
public for inspection.
FACTS
Fresno County has interpreted the phrase "heads of agencies," as found in Section
85700, to include the chief executive officer of each district in the County, thereby
requiring each head to file his or her statement of economic interests with the County
Board of Supervisors, zts the code reviewing body. The County is now seeking to change
the filing officer for agency heads for school and special districts to the districts
themselves.
' 
Gorrernment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear atittle 2, sections
18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
qsg


Grunwald, Barbara
August 22,2000
A-00-183
ANALYSIS
Section 87500 governs where statements of economic interests must be filed.
Subdivision (j) states that:
"Except as provided for in subdivision (k), heads of agencies,
members of boards or commissions not under a departrnent of state
government or members of boards or commissions not under the
jurisdiction of a local legislative body - one original with the
agency, which shall make and retain a copy and forward the
original to the code reviewing body which shall be the filing
officer. In its discretion, the code reviewing body may provide
that the original be filed directly with the code reviewing body and
that no copy be retained by the agency."
Section 82011(b) defines a code reviewing body as:
"The board of supervisors, with respect to the conflict-of-interest
code ofany county agency other than the board ofsupervisors, or
any agency of the judicial branch of government, and of any local
government agency, other than a city agency, with jurisdiction
wholly within the county."
Because the statute specifies where these statements mTst be filed, and no
statutory or regulatory exception is provided, the heads of agencies for county school and
special districts must continue to file with the Fresno County Board of Supervisors.
If you have any questions on this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
S. Moen, PhD
Political Reform Consultant II

QUESTION: May the Board of Supervisors issue a general letter to school districts and special
districts within Fresno County advising them that statements of economic interests for
"heads of agencies" for those districts may be retained by the district rather than being
submitted to the Board of Supervisors, as has been done in the past?

CONCLUSION: No. The statements of economic interests for county school and special districts
must be filed with the Fresno Count-v Board of Supervisors and made available to the
public for inspection.
